meta.title: Willow Psychology Practice | Registered Psychologist in Lindfield, Sydney
meta.description: Soo Lim, AHPRA-registered psychologist in Lindfield on Sydney's North Shore. Warm, evidence-based therapy for anxiety, depression, stress and relationships — in person or online. English & Korean. Medicare rebates available. Book a free 15-minute call.

jsonld.name: Willow Psychology Practice
jsonld.description: AHPRA-registered psychologist in Lindfield, Sydney. Warm, evidence-based therapy for anxiety, depression, stress and relationships, in person or via telehealth. Sessions in English and Korean.

hero.badge: Willow Psychology Practice · formerly Soo Psychology
hero.sub: Warm, evidence-based psychology in Lindfield and online, with Soo Lim — registered psychologist. Support for anxiety, low mood, stress and relationships, at your own pace.

intro.quote_html: I aim to offer a warm, non-judgemental space — to really listen, with care and curiosity, and to work alongside you at a pace that feels right.
intro.name: Soo Lim
intro.reg: AHPRA Reg: PSY0002187304 · SIRA Allied Health: 2004273

pain.title_html: If life has felt like too much lately…

cards.title: The worry won't stop
cards.body: Your mind won't switch off — racing thoughts, tension, that low hum of anxiety that follows you through the day.

cards.title: Flat, tired, stuck
cards.body: Low mood and no motivation. You're coping on the outside, but it's heavier than anyone around you knows.

cards.title: Things feel strained
cards.body: Tension in your relationships, or with yourself — patterns you can see but can't quite seem to shift alone.

problem.title_html: It isn't a lack of willpower — and it isn't your fault
problem.lede: When you're stuck, "just think positive" never reaches the real thing. A few honest reasons it's hard to shift on your own:

points.title: You're too close to it
points.body: It's hard to see your own patterns clearly from the inside. A trained, caring outside perspective changes what's visible.

points.title: Old patterns run deep
points.body: Ways of coping built up over years feel automatic. Lasting change needs the right tools, not simply more effort.

points.title: You've had nowhere safe
points.body: Somewhere genuinely confidential and non-judgemental — to say it out loud and be met with understanding — is rarer than it should be.

story.eyebrow: What it's like to work together

timeline.title: A space that's yours
timeline.body: We start by simply understanding what's going on for you — no pressure, no judgement, no script to follow.

timeline.title: Make sense of it
timeline.body: Using evidence-based approaches like CBT and ACT, we connect the dots between what you feel, think and do.

timeline.title: Tools you can use
timeline.body: You leave with practical strategies and a clearer, steadier sense of yourself — built around your goals, reviewed as you go.

solution.mark_html: Clinical skill with genuine human warmth — evidence-based therapy, always at your own pace.

how.title_html: Starting is simpler than you think
how.lede: Most people feel a little lighter just knowing what the first step is. Here it is:

steps.title: Book a free 15-min call
steps.body: A relaxed, no-obligation chat to see if we're a good fit and answer your questions. No referral needed for this step.

steps.title: Your first session
steps.body: We meet in Lindfield or online. You share what's bringing you in, and together we map out where to start.

steps.title: Ongoing support
steps.body: Regular sessions built around your goals and reviewed together. You stay in the driver's seat the whole way.

proof.lede: Reaching out takes courage. Here's what you can count on, from the very first moment.

cards.title: Completely confidential
cards.body: What you share stays private, within the clear legal and ethical limits a psychologist explains up front. This is your space.

cards.title: Qualified & accountable
cards.body: Soo is an AHPRA-registered psychologist, bound by professional standards — evidence-based care, never guesswork.

cards.title: Warm & non-judgemental
cards.body: No labels, no being "too much". Just steady, compassionate support that meets you exactly where you are.

proof.note: In line with AHPRA advertising guidelines, this practice does not publish client reviews or testimonials.

authority.body:
I provide a warm, non-judgemental space, and integrate evidence-based clinical practice with genuine, relational warmth. I work collaboratively with you — really listening, at a pace that feels right — to help you make the most of therapy.

Willow Psychology Practice is the new name for Soo Psychology — same psychologist, same care.
creds: Principal Registered Psychologist · AHPRA Reg PSY0002187304
Bachelor of Psychology (Honours), University of Sydney
In person in Lindfield · online across NSW

benefits.title: What we can work through together

items.title: Anxiety & stress
items.body: Racing thoughts, worry, panic, burnout and the feeling of being overwhelmed.

items.title: Depression & low mood
items.body: Low motivation, flatness, and finding your way back to feeling like you.

items.title: Relationships
items.body: Communication, conflict and boundaries — with others, and with yourself.

items.title: Self-esteem & identity
items.body: Self-criticism, confidence, and cross-cultural or identity questions.

items.title: Life transitions
items.body: Study, work, parenthood, moving countries and other big changes.

offer.title: Individual session
bullets: About $96.65 back with a Medicare rebate*
In person in Lindfield or online
offer.fine: *With a GP Mental Health Care Plan. Private health rebates may also apply. 24 hours' notice for changes.

target.title: Let's make sure it's right for you

yes.title: This could be a good fit
items: Feeling anxious, low, stressed or stuck and wanting support
You'd value a warm, collaborative, non-judgemental approach
You'd like in-person sessions in Lindfield, or online
You'd appreciate a psychologist who speaks English or Korean

no.title: Other support may suit better
items: In immediate danger or crisis — please call 000 or Lifeline 13 11 14 now
Looking only for medication management — that's a GP or psychiatrist
Needing a court-ordered or medico-legal assessment only

items.a: Not to book a free call or a private session. For a Medicare rebate you'll need a Mental Health Care Plan from your GP — I'm happy to guide you through how that works.
Individual sessions are $200, with about $96.65 back through a Medicare rebate. Couples sessions are $250. The first 15-minute phone call is free.
Yes. What you share is private, within the standard legal and ethical limits every psychologist explains at the start of working together.
Yes — secure telehealth sessions are available across NSW, as well as in person in Lindfield.

items.q: What happens in the first session?
items.a: Mostly listening. You share what's bringing you in, ask anything you like, and together we decide where to start. There's no pressure.

items.q: Do you offer sessions in Korean?
items.a: Yes — Soo works in both English and Korean. 한국어 상담도 편하게 가능합니다.

items.q: How do I know if therapy is for me?
items.a: That's exactly what the free 15-minute call is for — a no-obligation way to see if it feels right before you commit to anything.

booking.lede: Choose in person in Lindfield or online via telehealth, and pick a time that suits you. Secure booking through Halaxy.

booking.note: Bookings are handled securely by Halaxy. Prefer to talk first? Call 0423 085 723 to arrange a free 15-minute consultation.

final.body: There's no rush and no pressure — just a calm, confidential space whenever you're ready. The easiest way to begin is a free 15-minute call.

info.body_html: Call 000 if in immediate danger. Urgent support: Lifeline 13 11 14 · Beyond Blue 1300 22 4636.

Shop 2, First Floor
356 Pacific Hwy, Lindfield NSW

footer.line: Willow Psychology Practice — formerly Soo Psychology · Soo Lim, Principal Registered Psychologist · AHPRA PSY0002187304 · Lindfield NSW
footer.disclaimer: Willow Psychology Practice provides general psychological services and is not a crisis or emergency service. If you are in immediate danger, call 000, or Lifeline 13 11 14. In line with AHPRA advertising guidelines, this site does not publish client testimonials or reviews. Information here is general and not a substitute for personalised clinical advice.
